title: myrtus calycolpoides subsp. jackii
intro: Myrtus calycolpoides subsp. jackii, also known as Jack's myrtle, is a subspecies of shrub or small tree in the myrtle family, native to India and Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Jack's myrtle include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant protection. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Jack's myrtle are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
